source: TN State Planning Commission — Peri-Urban Management Study 2026

urbanization.note: Census 2011 base data. CBUAs delineated using DEGURBA + satellite imagery.

key_findings: Hosur has highest peri-urban growth share (75.5%) — driven by Bengaluru spillover
Salem and Thanjavur show 60%+ peri-urban growth — industrial corridor effect
Chennai has lowest peri-urban share (29.5%) despite largest absolute growth — mature urban core
37% of built-up area lies outside statutory town boundaries — governance gap
490 town panchayats = 74% of all ULBs — most urban governance at smallest scale
policy_recommendations: Establish Rural-to-Urban Transition Agencies at state and district levels
Mandate community participation in merger decisions when reclassifying rural→urban
Enable coordinated taxation across urban-rural boundaries for peri-urban areas
Integrate peri-urban areas into master plans of adjoining corporations
Protect agricultural land in peri-urban zones through zoning regulations
